i-3ILpkyelnH.bio_short: Maria is a PhD student at Oxford working on applications of persistent homology to biological data analysis, alongside the theory of multiparameter persistence. Her research aims to bridge rigorous mathematics with biological understanding through interpretable and transparent methods.

i-4LID9SJJLc.bio_short: An applied mathematician interested in dynamical systems, chemical reaction network theory, topological data analysis, and systems biology.
i-4LID9SJJLc.title: Dr.

i-5JhnTb06rJ.bio_short: Vincenzo works in algebraic geometry at the edge of representation theory, with a particular focus on tensors and their algebraic varieties — secant varieties, Hilbert schemes of points, and varieties enriched with group actions.

i-6C4yJsmsSW.bio_short: A Postdoctoral Research Associate at the Mathematical Insititute, Oxford. His research focuses on theoretical aspects of topological data analysis and its interactions with various areas of geometry, analysis and dynamics.
i-6C4yJsmsSW.title: Dr.

i-8GFXYXc870.bio_short: PhD student at the University of Oxford interested in analyzing spatial relationships through algebraic topology.

i-AQRxMaIlMI.bio_short: Matthias Arzt is a Research Software Engineer at the Max Planck Institute of Molecular Cell Biology and Genetics (MPI-CBG) in Dresden. He develops and maintains computational tools for biological image analysis, with a focus on image segmentation and cell tracking. Matthias contributes to open-source projects such as ImageJ, Labkit and Mastodon, supporting scientists in extracting quantitative insights from complex microscopy data.

publications.Publication title: Topology identifies concurrent cyclic processes in single-cell transcriptomics and androgen receptor function

i-AecZQSk2p6.bio_short: Katherine is a DPhil student at Oxford working on applications of topology to spatial biology. She is especially interested in multiparameter persistent homology, and methods for single-cell and spatial transcriptomics.

i-BLqvm5ExuR.bio_short: Lucy's research focuses on developing and applying Topological Data Analysis to the analysis of spatial sub-cellular biological data. I completed my Masters in mathematics at the University of Oxford, and researched on supersymmetric quantum field theory.

i-Lyudz32Y-G.bio_short: A cancer genomics scientist turned science communicator, Anna brings rare dual fluency in research and its public life to the MPI Center for Systems Biology.

He has also worked with curvature measures on networks, and has a strong interest in mathematical outreach.

i-SrkpsoPzq-.bio_short: Nikola's research focuses on developing topological methods to address problems in discrete geometry and combinatorics, as well as investigating questions in algebraic and applied topology. He loves discussing math with people from different backgrounds, where different viewpoints can reveal surprising connections.

i-YB8Eg4RDLG.bio_short: Adi's research interests include topological singularities in data, computational derived sheaf theory, and representations of Lie groups. Their current research involves cellular microlocal geometry and its applications to topological data analysis.

i-aV5LmwqN0n.bio_short: Lida's work explores how topology and applied mathematics can shape the future of neuroscience — combining topological data analysis, machine learning, and computational modelling to study brain morphologies, understand pathologies, and improve diagnostic tools.

i-dsCt4S6lGo.bio_short: A Director at MPI-CBG, Dresden and Professor of Mathematics at the University of Oxford. Her research develops new mathematical frameworks in algebra, topology, and dynamical systems to extract structure and meaning from complex biological data.

i-g0slIejKR6.bio_short: Leon is a PhD student working on multiparameter persistent homology, chromatic persistence, and persistent cycle representatives. His interests span both the mathematical theory and computational implementation of topological methods for analysing biological datasets and images.

Identifiability in Graphical Discrete Lyapunov Models

i-iJVJHewIbN.bio_short: Silviana works on problems in applied numerical algebraic geometry and scientific computation.  She most enjoys finding the x, such that f(x)=0.  Her favorite fields are of characteristic 0.  Her favorite programming languages are C++ and Python, though she always enjoys learning new ones.  Before joining Algebraic Systems Biology in Dresden, she was Associate Professor of Mathematics at UWEC in Wisconsin.

i-lBbAGOg7jM.title: Dr.

i-oL2k5Ov2fY.bio_short: Juan works in algebraic topology and homotopy theory, with a current focus on configuration spaces and their homotopy type — a line of enquiry directly motivated by molecular biology. His goal is to extend classical results from configuration space theory into a form that can be applied to biological systems.

i-o_D5od6p6o.bio_short: Lukas' research interests sits within the triangle of homotopy theory, stratified spaces, and topological data analysis — and anything that happens in the spaces between them.
i-o_D5od6p6o.title: Dr.

i-pm_jpVzopL.bio_short: Rob studies the topology of mathematical models which describe complex processes in the life sciences. Working with simulated and experimental data, Rob compares different modelling approaches in terms of their spatial and temporal properties. Co-supervised with Professor Helen Byrne.

i-yDgObOpSCJ.bio_short: Kelly's mathematical interests run from the abstract to the urgently applied: rational homotopy theory and model categories at the pure end; persistence and topological data analysis in the middle; geometric deep learning — a field Kelly approaches with the conviction that message-passing architectures should be replaced entirely by neural k-forms; and single cell RNA sequencing at the applied end. Current projects span all of it.

publications.Publication title: Topology identifies concurrent cyclic processes in single-cell transcriptomics and androgen receptor function
Cell decompositions of persistent minimal models
Interval-sphere model structures

i-yDgObOpSCJ.title: Dr.

i-yPPhHEKjsE.bio_short: Florian works on the mathematics of shape and structure in data. His tools come from topology and sheaf theory, pushed into the new territory of multiparameter persistence.
